content.full_text:
Robert C. Summers                                    July 23, 1976
Franchise Tax Board


Rich Eichman
Lobbyist Audits


The Commission staff has reviewed your memo of June 29, 1976 and
issued the following responses:

     1.  Yes

     2.  Yes, if there is evidence that the lobbyist may have qualified
         as a lobbyist in a month prior to the month of his first re-
         port.

Please contact me if you have any further questions.




cc:  John Gordnier
     Deputy Attorney General

bcc: Jeanne P.
     Bob Stern
     Rich Eichman

State of California                                                 Franchise Tax Board

Memorandum

To    :  Michael Bennett, Executive Director          Date  :  June 29, 1976
         Fair Political Practices Commission
         1100 K Street
         Sacramento, CA  95814                         File No.:  DQ:1r


From  :  Robert C. Summers

Subject:  Questions Concerning Lobbyist Audits
          Your memo of June 10, 1976


         We have reviewed your answers to the questions we submitted and there is
         one point that needs further clarification.

         Your answer to our Question 3B, pertaining to the records which are
         subject to audit, dealt only with the situation where all the records
         were used to prepare the monthly report.

         We need further clarification in this area.  Please answer the following
         questions:

         (a)  The lobbyist qualifies on June 15 and includes only the
              transactions from June 15 to the end of the month on his
              monthly report.  Are the records for the transactions
              from June 1 to June 14 subject to audit?

         (b)  If an individual qualifies as a lobbyist in June and
              registers, are his records for the time prior to June
              subject to audit?  The auditor would examine them to
              determine the correct date the lobbyist qualified.

         Your reply to these questions will help us to clarify which records are
         subject to audit.


         [signature]

         Chief, Political Reform Audit Division

         cc:  Martin Huff

[The image appears to be a very faint or poorly scanned document where the text is largely illegible. While there are clear indications of typed text throughout the page, the content is too faint to read with confidence and accuracy. Without being able to reliably make out the specific words and passages, I cannot provide a faithful verbatim transcription as requested.]
